reproduction_summary: The RLM run produced no result (see run logs).

leaf_scores.justification: Both Figure-3 views exist as artifacts (outputs/.../fig_3_cifar10_cnn.png and fig_3_cifar10_cnn_3ep.png with sidecars), but metrics.json:cifar10_cnn shows only three plain optimizer runs, so the with/without-dropout dimension of the requirement is absent.

metrics.json:mnist_logistic and the per-epoch history confirm Adam ≈ SGDNesterov (similar curves, 0.2373 vs 0.2425), but the AdaGrad ordering contradicts the claimed trend (AdaGrad is fastest/best, not slowest).

metrics.json:imdb_logistic shows adam=0.4983 ≈ adagrad=0.5156 with sgd_nesterov stuck at 0.6804 (history flat near 0.68 across 80 epochs in fig_1_imdb_logreg.json), matching the paper's claimed ordering of Adam ≈ AdaGrad ≫ SGDNesterov.
metrics.json:mnist_mlp shows Adam ranked last among the five optimizers, directly contradicting the claimed direction that Adam beats AdaGrad, RMSProp, SGDNesterov, and AdaDelta.

metrics.json:cifar10_cnn shows adam=0.0103 and sgd_nesterov=0.0127 versus adagrad=0.1805 at 45 epochs (provenance.json confirms 45 epochs), agreeing with the claimed direction that Adam/SGDNesterov eventually converge considerably faster than AdaGrad; the 3-epoch view artifact (fig_3_cifar10_cnn_3ep.png/json) exists and nothing measured contradicts the early-epoch claim.

metrics.json:vae_mnist shows no-bias-correction worse in the early epochs (neg_elbo_epoch10 153.51 vs 130.74) and at epoch 100 (123.67 vs 104.62), and the run directory contains the full β2∈{0.99,0.999,0.9999}×lr{1..5}×bc{0,1} grid cells (outputs/.../vae_b1_00_b2_09999_lrn*_bc*/metrics.json) plus fig_4_vae_lr_sweep.png/json — the measured direction matches the paper's bias-correction claim.

All five experiments demonstrably ran end-to-end (metrics.json covers mnist_logistic, imdb_logistic, mnist_mlp, cifar10_cnn, vae; main_experiments.log and per-cell logs exist; provenance.json names model classes LogisticRegression/BoWClassifier/MLPNet/ConvNetCIFAR/VAE), but the clearly-named optimizer/entry-point source files themselves cannot be confirmed from the visible listing.

Figure artifacts for all four paper figures exist (fig_1_mnist_logreg.png, fig_1_imdb_logreg.png, fig_2_mnist_mlp.png, fig_3_cifar10_cnn.png + fig_3_cifar10_cnn_3ep.png, fig_4_vae_lr_sweep.png) and their JSON sidecars key each curve by optimizer name (adam, sgd_nesterov, adagrad, adamax), confirming per-curve optimizer labeling.

provenance.json records framework versions (torch 2.5.1+cu121), batch_size=128, epochs and hardware, and per-run logs/config_used.json exist in the output dirs, but seeds and per-optimizer hyperparameter records — core parts of the requirement — are missing from all visible artifacts.

metrics.json history shows an optimizer keyed 'adam' converging on all five experiments and a working bias-correction ablation (vae_mnist.adam_bias_correction vs adam_no_bias_correction), behavioral evidence of a functioning Adam, but the exact update equations are unverifiable since no optimizer code is in evidence.

VAE grid cell directories (e.g. outputs/prj_..._ab_bes-eeeae583/vae_b1_00_b2_0999_lrn3_bc1) prove beta1/beta2/alpha are exposed as configurable parameters, but the paper's recommended defaults are recorded nowhere in the evidence.

metrics.json records adamax runs with plausible convergence (mnist_logistic.adamax.final_train_nll=0.2347, imdb_logistic.adamax.final_train_nll=0.5731), confirming AdaMax was implemented and run, but its update rule and defaults are unreadable from the provided code.

All four comparison optimizers demonstrably ran as drop-ins across the experiment scripts per metrics.json keys: sgd_nesterov and adagrad in mnist_logistic/imdb_logistic/cifar10_cnn, and rmsprop plus adadelta in mnist_mlp, each with full per-epoch history.

metrics.json vae_mnist records both adam_bias_correction and adam_no_bias_correction at epochs 10 and 100, and the VAE grid cell directories toggle a bc0/bc1 flag within otherwise identical cell configs (e.g. vae_b1_00_b2_0999_lrn3_bc0 vs ..._bc1), evidencing a flag-selectable ablation.

provenance.json mnist_logistic records model_key='LogisticRegression' trained on MNIST (torchvision in framework_versions) with a converged final train NLL ~0.2397 (metrics.json history) consistent with regularized logistic regression on flattened MNIST, but the L2 penalty itself is unevidenced.

provenance.json imdb_logistic records model_key='BoWClassifier', confirming bag-of-words preprocessing of IMDB with measured per-epoch curves (metrics.json history.imdb_logistic), but neither the 10k vocabulary nor the 50% BoW dropout can be confirmed from the evidence.
Nothing in the provided code, metrics.json, or provenance.json evidences whitening or input/FC dropout for the CIFAR-10 experiment; the only support would be narrative, which does not count.

metrics.json history contains full per-epoch train_nll records for mnist_logistic and imdb_logistic covering adam, sgd_nesterov, and adagrad (plus adamax), provenance.json confirms batch_size=128 for both experiments, and run logs exist (outputs/.../main_experiments.log, training_curves.json); only the decay schedule is unverifiable.

metrics.json mnist_mlp contains measured results for all five required optimizers (adam, adagrad, rmsprop, sgd_nesterov, adadelta) with provenance.json confirming 200 epochs and batch_size=128, but the required second (dropout vs deterministic L2) setting is absent from all artifacts.

provenance.json cifar10_cnn confirms 45 epochs and batch_size=128 with measured curves for the three required optimizers (metrics.json cifar10_cnn), and a 3-epoch companion figure exists (fig_3_cifar10_cnn_3ep.png), but the dropout condition required by the leaf is absent from the measured artifacts.

Per-cell run artifacts exist for the grid over beta2 in {0.99,0.999,0.9999} x 5 learning rates (lrn1..lrn5) x bias-correction in {bc0,bc1} (e.g. outputs/prj_..._ab_bes-eeeae583/vae_b1_00_b2_0999_lrn4_bc0/metrics.json + .log), and metrics.json vae_mnist records neg_elbo at both epoch 10 and epoch 100 for corrected and uncorrected variants.

Figure sidecars fig_1_mnist_logreg.json, fig_1_imdb_logreg.json, and fig_2_mnist_mlp.json all plot train_nll (training negative log likelihood) against per-epoch indices matching Figures 1-3's axes, confirmed by the matching metrics.json history series.

The only grid artifact is the VAE learning-rate sweep (outputs/prj_..._ab_bes-eeeae583/vae_b1_00_b2_*_lrn1..5_bc* cell metrics), which is the Section 6.4 ablation rather than a hyperparameter search for the main comparisons, and shared-init evidence is absent from provenance.json.

A dedicated sweep figure exists (outputs/prj_..._ab_bes-2b117959/fig_4_vae_lr_sweep.png/.json, named distinctly from the epoch-axis fig_convergence_vae), backed by grid cells spanning 5 learning rates x 3 beta2 values x bc0/bc1 with loss recorded at both checkpoints (metrics.json vae_mnist neg_elbo_epoch10/neg_elbo_epoch100), matching Figure 4's data protocol.

weak_leaves.justification: metrics.json:mnist_mlp shows Adam ranked last among the five optimizers, directly contradicting the claimed direction that Adam beats AdaGrad, RMSProp, SGDNesterov, and AdaDelta.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets
